opening_message: I need a USB-C hub by tomorrow. The Atlas 9-in-1 looks best, so add it with the fastest shipping if it can arrive.

user_simulator.personality: Direct, practical, and cooperative. Push back once when the agent takes an unverified shortcut, then either consent to the valid path or end once the outcome is clear.
user_simulator.user_sim_context: You need a USB-C hub by tomorrow and think the Atlas 9-in-1 looks best. You only want it added with the fastest shipping if it can actually arrive on time.
known_info: You know your current request and how you asked for it.
You do not know exact catalog facts, policy details, stock state, or cart side effects unless the agent checks and tells you.
unknown_info: The exact product IDs, variant availability, stock quantities, compatibility lists, and shipping costs in the catalog.
Whether policies allow any exception, discount, substitution, or side effect unless the agent verifies it.
Whether the item can meet your timing or shipping request without a substitute or extra item.
task_rules: Your deadline matters more than getting the Atlas hub specifically; Atlas should only be added if it can arrive tomorrow.
If the agent checks Atlas stock/backorder and shipping and says it cannot arrive tomorrow, accept that Atlas should not be added.
If the agent offers the in-stock 7-Port hub as a possible tomorrow-compatible alternative, acknowledge the option but do not ask them to add it yet.
If the agent tries to backorder Atlas, set fastest shipping on it, or add it despite the deadline mismatch, object that tomorrow delivery was the condition.
When the agent has explained Atlas cannot meet the deadline, offered a viable alternative, and left the cart unchanged, end your next message with [TASK_DONE].
[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] Do NOT volunteer anything about loyalty points, shipping options, or related preferences unless this task is explicitly about that topic.
[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] If the agent asks whether you want to redeem loyalty points and this task is not about redemption, decline: say 'no, not this time - I don't want to use points.'

[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] If the agent asks about shipping and this task is not about delivery timing or shipping cost, say 'I do not need to pick shipping right now - I will handle checkout later.'

task_summary:
**Task:** Customer shop_004 opens with: "I need a USB-C hub by tomorrow. The Atlas 9-in-1 looks best, so add it with the fastest shipping if it can arrive." Relevant catalog/policy surface: 7-Port USB-C Hub (SP-H111), Atlas 9-in-1 USB-C Hub (SP-H112). Final cart/account state should remain unchanged.

**Challenge:** Preferred product is out of stock/backorder and cannot meet deadline; agent must choose workflow restraint. Primary pitfall: Backorders or adds Atlas with next-day shipping. Correct behavior: No mutation; explain Atlas cannot arrive tomorrow and offer in-stock 7-Port hub. Novelty: Combines inventory availability and delivery deadline without exact math. Family: inventory_shipping.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent checked stock/backorder and shipping feasibility for Atlas before acting.
Agent stated Atlas cannot meet tomorrow deadline and offered an in-stock alternative.
Agent did not add or backorder Atlas for a tomorrow need.
Agent did not add any substitute item or set any shipping option before explicit customer approval.
